headline: Home Depot, CAVA earnings, Housing data top Tuesday watchlist
summary: Asking for a Trend, host Josh Lipton breaks down the top stories investors will be watching on Tuesday, May 19.On the earnings front, Home Depot (HD) reports quarterly results before the bell, followed by Toll Brothers (TOL) and CAVA (CAVA) after the close.Investors will also be watching fresh housing data with the release of the latest Pending Home Sales report.

content.title: Home Depot, CAVA earnings, Housing data top Tuesday watchlist
content.description:
Asking for a Trend, host Josh Lipton breaks down the top stories investors will be watching on Tuesday, May 19.

On the earnings front, Home Depot (HD) reports quarterly results before the bell, followed by Toll Brothers (TOL) and CAVA (CAVA) after the close.

Investors will also be watching fresh housing data with the release of the latest Pending Home Sales report.
content.summary: Asking for a Trend, host Josh Lipton breaks down the top stories investors will be watching on Tuesday, May 19.On the earnings front, Home Depot (HD) reports quarterly results before the bell, followed by Toll Brothers (TOL) and CAVA (CAVA) after the close.Investors will also be watching fresh housing data with the release of the latest Pending Home Sales report.

headline: Home Depot profit falls, but it tops expectations in the face of growing economic uncertainty
summary: Home Depot got a lift in the first quarter from professionals and also homeowners stocking up on spring supplies.  Profit fell from its first quarter last year, but the national home improvement retailer beat Wall Street expectations.  "The underlying demand in our business was relatively similar to what we saw throughout fiscal 2025, despite greater consumer uncertainty and housing affordability pressure,” CEO Ted Decker said Tuesday.

content.title: Home Depot profit falls, but it tops expectations in the face of growing economic uncertainty
content.summary: Home Depot got a lift in the first quarter from professionals and also homeowners stocking up on spring supplies.  Profit fell from its first quarter last year, but the national home improvement retailer beat Wall Street expectations.  "The underlying demand in our business was relatively similar to what we saw throughout fiscal 2025, despite greater consumer uncertainty and housing affordability pressure,” CEO Ted Decker said Tuesday.

thumbnail.caption: FILE - A view of the exterior of the Home Depot improvement store, in Niles, Ill., on Feb. 19, 2022. (AP Photo/Nam Y. Huh, File)

headline: The Home Depot Announces First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results; Reaffirms Fiscal 2026 Guidance
summary: The Home Depot®, the world's largest home improvement retailer, today reported sales of $41.8 billion for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, an increase of $1.9 billion, or 4.8% from the first quarter of fiscal 2025. Comparable sales for the first quarter of fiscal 2026 increased 0.6%, and comparable sales in the U.S. increased 0.4%. For the first quarter of fiscal 2026, foreign exchange rates positively impacted total company comparable sales by approximately 55 basis points.

content.title: The Home Depot Announces First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results; Reaffirms Fiscal 2026 Guidance
content.summary: The Home Depot®, the world's largest home improvement retailer, today reported sales of $41.8 billion for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, an increase of $1.9 billion, or 4.8% from the first quarter of fiscal 2025. Comparable sales for the first quarter of fiscal 2026 increased 0.6%, and comparable sales in the U.S. increased 0.4%. For the first quarter of fiscal 2026, foreign exchange rates positively impacted total company comparable sales by approximately 55 basis points.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: ServiceNow, Home Depot, Agilysys, Sandisk, Nvidia, Alphabet, and More
summary: Stocks were falling Tuesday and the tech-led rally continued to lose momentum as government bonds sharply sold off with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent calling on U.S. allies to move against Iran.  Home Depot  declined 1.3% after the home-improvement retailer posted first-quarter earnings that beat analysts’ estimates and revenue rose 4.8% to $41.8 billion, with comparable sales up 0.6%.  ServiceNow  gained 5.4% to $109.23 to lead the  The software stock was building on its 9% jump in the previous session after Bank of America reinstated coverage of the software company with a Buy rating and a $130 price target.

ServiceNow  gained 5.4% to $109.23 to lead the  The software stock was building on its 9% jump in the previous session after Bank of America reinstated coverage of the software company with a Buy rating and a $130 price target.

headline: Home Depot flags choppy demand for big remodels as uncertainty weighs
summary:
STORY: Home Depot on Tuesday said Americans are scaling back on large home remodeling projects as they grapple with economic uncertainty and a subdued housing market.

:: Home Depot handout

The big box retailer kicked off a big week of corporate earnings results by beating both sales and profit estimates, but warned that its customers remain cautious.

U.S. consumer sentiment slumped to a record low in early May as the war in Iran fanned inflation pressures and strained household finances.

That's kept spending on home improvement projects muted amid elevated mortgage rates and high home prices.

:: Home Depot handout

Going forward, Home Depot expects both sales and profit to be in the range of flat to a small rise.

The company said elevated fuel costs are also pressuring the business through both transportation and input expenses, but noted that tariff refunds could help significantly.

Results from fellow U.S. retailers this week are likely to shed more light on the state of the consumer.

Rival Lowe's and big-box retailer Target are set to report on Wednesday, followed on Thursday by sector bellwether Walmart.

content.title: Home Depot flags choppy demand for big remodels as uncertainty weighs

headline: Stock Market Today: Dow, Nasdaq End In The Red As Yields Jump; Alphabet Drops On I/O Day (Live Coverage)
summary: The major stock indexes finished in negative territory Tuesday as inflation worries drove up Treasury yields, elevating borrowing costs for companies and consumers. The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq composite each fell for a third straight session after recent record highs.
